findings.summary: a 160000 gitlink drove a FAIL, false-FAILing a legitimate standalone brain with its own history (the #236 finding); a gitlink is recorded only when the embedded repo has commits, so it is no longer a FAIL trigger — only entries-in-history/index is
the 'gitignore it to silence this' escape hatch did not work on a tracked path; check-ignore now uses --no-index
a real git submodule brain FAILed with the destructive remedy; now detected via 160000 + .gitmodules and left alone
q() swallowed git failures, downgrading a real FAIL to a misleading WARN on a lock/timeout; a rev-parse liveness probe now gates a SKIP via the injectable opts.git seam (RFC-024 §1)
the 160000 and inHistory clauses had zero coverage; every clause now reverts red, inHistory isolated from inIndex via a git-rm--cached fixture
brain dir == repo root: <brainDir>/.git is the PROJECT's git, not an embedded brain, and the remedy said rm the project's history; now skipped when resolve(brainDir)===resolve(toplevel)
FAIL asserted an unverifiable cause ('an older build created it'); removed
tests use absolute brainDir while the CLI passes relative; a doctor-wide pre-existing property of the relative default, correct in the normal case, not introduced here

ruledOut: read-only: every probe (rev-parse, check-ignore --no-index, config --file .gitmodules, ls-files, log) verified non-writing; realGit rejects fetch
no new false positives across worktree, monorepo inner package, nested repos, brain outside repo, submodule — all verdicts correct through the real CLI

the proof can fail: all 7 clauses mutation-tested red, verified live on every shape (standalone+160000 WARN, tracked+gitignored silent, submodule silent, canonical WARN, corrupted FAIL, git-locked SKIP, brain==root silent)
issue #77: the check never prescribes vfkb init and has no plugin coupling
